records.ety: [F. rprimande, fr. L. reprimendus, reprimenda, that is to be checked or suppressed, fr. reprimere to check, repress; pref. re- re + premere to press. See Press, and cf. Repress.]

defs: Severe or formal reproof; reprehension, private or public.
quotes: Goldsmith gave his landlady a sharp reprimand for her treatment of him.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Reprimanded; p. pr. & vb. n. Reprimanding.]

defs: To reprove severely; to reprehend; to chide for a fault; to consure formally.
quotes: Germanicus was severely reprimanded by Tiberius for traveling into Egypt without his permission.

defs: To reprove publicly and officially, in execution of a sentence; as, the court ordered him to be reprimanded.
